topic.what: Six hybrid KEM combiner designs pairing FrodoKEM (unstructured LWE) with ML-KEM (module-LWE) for IND-CCA2 security when either assumption is hard
topic.how: Structural-splicing and hardened variants of textbook combiners (parallel HKDF, SHAKE256 split-key, sequential chaining, XOR, nested) with uniform robustness predicate
topic.why_matters: Provides post-quantum security with hybrid robustness at minimal overhead; practical deployment via liboqs benchmarks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance overhead introduced by combiner relative to slower constituent
characteristics.evidence: Table 8.3; Panther-SS combiner-only overhead below half a percent of total latency
characteristics.context: Panther-SS achieves full robustness with combiner-only overhead below 0.5%

characteristics.value: IND-CCA2 when either underlying assumption holds
characteristics.evidence: Market-Theoretic Security Framework proofs documented by bidding rounds; Master Splitting Lemma for OR-security
characteristics.context: V-security (OR-security) when either FrodoKEM or ML-KEM assumption is hard

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance ranking relative to NIST PQC candidates
characteristics.evidence: Section 8.11; security-vs-performance Pareto analysis against Kyber/ML-KEM FrodoNTRU SABER HQC BIKE Classic-McEliece
characteristics.context: Panther family sits on Pareto frontier of post-quantum KEM candidates
Hybrid construction combines both ciphertexts increasing total size

characteristics.what_it_measures: Resistance to implementation attacks on constituents
characteristics.evidence: Section 7; Attack-vs-Defence Matrix; quantitative leakage comparison
characteristics.context: Design choices block published side-channel attacks on both constituents

novelty: novel structural-splicing combiner Panther-SS interleaving ciphertexts and binding cut-positions via structural tag
market-theoretic security framework with bidding-round proofs for systematic security documentation
six robust hybrid KEM combiners with uniform robustness predicate (transcript binding domain separation implicit rejection length normalisation OR-security)
comprehensive benchmarking against all NIST PQC Rounds 1-4 candidates and side-channel attack matrix
open_problems: practical side-channel resistance in deployed implementations
adoption barriers for hybrid schemes in production protocols
scalability analysis for unbounded session counts
